messages.content:
You write Python docstrings. You will receive one Python source file and a list
of target symbols (functions, methods, classes) that lack docstrings. Respond
with a single JSON object and nothing else:
{"docstrings": {"<qualname>": "<docstring text>", ...}}
Rules: one entry per target, no extra keys. Docstrings must be plain text
(no surrounding quotes), accurate to the code, and at least 20 non-whitespace characters.
For every function or method every parameter name, excluding self and cls, must
appear verbatim in that target's docstring. Copy keys exactly as given in the
required keys list. Do not restate the code. Do not include reasoning.

message.content: {"docstrings": {"CloseSpider": "An extension that forces spiders to be closed after certain conditions are met.", "CloseSpider.from_crawler": "Create an instance of CloseSpider from the crawler crawler.", "CloseSpider.error_count": "Handle spider error signals to track failure and response counts.", "CloseSpider.page_count": "Track the number of response and request objects received by the spider.", "CloseSpider.spider_opened": "Initialize the timeout task when the spider is opened.", "CloseSpider.item_scraped": "Increment the item count for the spider when an item is scraped.", "CloseSpider.spider_closed": "Clean up tasks when the spider is closed.", "CloseSpider.spider_opened_no_item": "Start a looping call to monitor item production for the spider.", "CloseSpider.item_scraped_no_item": "Update the item count for the spider when an item is scraped without a timeout."}}
